title: rapuntium inflatum
side_effects: Rapuntium inflatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as excessive skin irritation, severe allergic reactions, and skin discoloration. Ingesting the plant's sap can cause nausea, vomiting, and stomach cramps. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile compounds can lead to respiratory problems, including wheezing and shortness of breath. Improper use of Rapuntium inflatum can also result in eye damage, including blurred vision and tearing.
